agent_a.description: 4 models (minimax-m27, minimax-m25, lm-coder-40b, lm-qwen-9b) x 9 domains

agent_b.description: 6 models (granite-1b, granite-350m, gemma, qwen35, phi4, lfm) x 9 domains (2 combos missing)

telemetry_minimax.description: Detailed telemetry for minimax-m27 across 9 domains (separate test run)

details.agent_b_only: 52 runs (6 models x 9, missing 2 combos), 11 passes = 21.2%
details.agent_b_expected_54: 6 models x 9 domains = 54 expected, but 2 combos missing (qwen35/hydra, qwen35/remotion)
details.analysis: The 4 successes and p5-only pattern match Agent A exactly. The '54 runs' figure (9x6) matches Agent B's test matrix dimensions, not Agent A's (9x4=36). The plan appears to conflate Agent A's scoring results (4 passes, all p5, all 0.68) with Agent B's test matrix dimensions (6 models). Neither dataset independently produces 4/54. The 7.4% figure is not directly reproducible from any single dataset.

dead_zone_068.claimed: all p5 scores = 0.68 (CreativeEvaluator structural checkboxes, no quality discrimination)

additional_evidence.code_size_discrepancy: minimax models: 160 bytes (tiny stubs), lm models: 2163 bytes (full code) -- BOTH scored 0.68
additional_evidence.telemetry_html_score: HTML domain also scored 0.65 (different from 0.68, so not ALL domains)

dead_zone_068.notes: The core claim is VERIFIED: CreativeEvaluator gives identical 0.68 scores to p5 code regardless of actual quality. 160-byte stubs score the same as 2163-byte full implementations. This confirms no quality discrimination. The 'Dead Zone' label is accurate.

ralphloop_iter1.claimed: every successful run stopped at iteration 1

details.telemetry_evidence: logs/telemetry-minimax-m27.jsonl shows iterations: 2 for a SEPARATE minimax test (with maxIterations: 5 in dogfood-minimax-m27.ts)
details.quality_gate_logic: RalphLoop quality gate only triggers at iteration >= 2, meaning with maxIterations: 1, the gate never fires
details.actual_mechanism: The loop stops at iteration 0 (the only iteration) because maxIterations=1 forces it, NOT because the quality threshold was met
details.important_distinction: The plan's claim implies 0.68 was treated as 'good enough' by RalphLoop. In reality, the test harness forced iteration=1, preventing the quality gate from ever evaluating the score. With maxIterations >= 3, the quality gate WOULD reject 0.68 (< 0.7 threshold) at iteration 2.

ralphloop_iter1.notes: Every successful Agent A run did stop at 1 iteration, but this was a test harness limitation (maxIterations: 1), not a RalphLoop quality assessment. The claim that 'Loop treats 0.68 as quality threshold met and exits' is INCORRECT -- the loop never evaluated quality at all. A more accurate framing: 'test harness limited to 1 iteration, preventing RalphLoop from applying quality gate'.

sessions_lost_per_category.claimed: 6+ sessions lost per category in token waste analysis

details.source: Derived from archaeological session analysis, not dogfood test results
details.note: These are qualitative estimates from session transcript analysis. The 6+ figure represents expert judgment about how many sessions were affected, not a countable metric from test data. Cannot be verified or falsified from the dogfood result files alone.

sessions_lost_per_category.notes: The session count estimates are qualitative archaeological findings, not dogfood test results. Verification would require recounting affected sessions from the 58 session transcripts, which is outside the scope of dogfood result file analysis.

agent_a_errors_categorized.no_llm_configured: 16 of 32 failures (minimax models on non-p5 domains)
agent_a_errors_categorized.validation_failed: 12 failures (lm models on non-p5 domains, cross-contamination with p5 validation)

cross_contamination_examples: Three.js code rejected with 'p5.js code must contain at least one of: function'
Strudel code rejected with 'p5.js code must contain at least one of: function'
Hydra code rejected with 'p5.js code must contain at least one of: function'
Tone.js code rejected with 'p5.js code must contain at least one of: function'
HTML code from granite-350m rejected with 'p5.js code must contain at least one of: function'

verified_claims: 0.68 Dead Zone (confirmed: identical scores regardless of code quality)
partially_verified: 7.4% success rate (4 successes confirmed in Agent A, but 54-run denominator conflates two datasets)
RalphLoop iter=1 (confirmed stop at 1 iteration, but due to maxIterations:1 in harness, not quality assessment)
unverifiable_from_data: 6+ sessions lost per category (qualitative session estimate, not in dogfood data)
summary.key_insight: The plan's dogfood section accurately describes the symptoms (0.68 dead zone, p5-only success, validation cross-contamination) but misattributes the iteration-1 behavior. RalphLoop did not 'treat 0.68 as good enough' -- the test harness was configured to run only 1 iteration, preventing the quality gate from ever evaluating. The 54-run denominator appears to be Agent B's test matrix dimensions (6x9) applied to Agent A's results (4x9=36).
